methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $41M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
First Resonance, a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $40.8M to date, is valued within a frontier-tech sector populated by 8 private peers and 1 public comparable. The peer universe spans valuations from $300M to $10.0B, with a median of $1.6B across 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies including 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Foundation Robotics ($3.0B, Series A), and Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $40.8M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a $82M valuation. Peer-median analysis directly references the $1.6B median across the identified 7 comparable companies, reflecting the sector's current valuation environment.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $69M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $866M. This wide spread reflects material uncertainty: the peer set exhibits extreme dispersion ($300M–$10.0B), and First Resonance's exact competitive positioning within robotics and manufacturing—as well as revenue scale, product maturity, and market traction relative to peers—remains underspecified in available data. Stage-progression implies a conservative floor; peer-median better captures sector-wide appetite but requires clarity on First Resonance's differentiation relative to both high-valuation ($10B) and lower-valuation ($500M) comparables.
